content: Hi, I'm having a lot of trouble with my internet today. It keeps dropping in and out.

Sorry you're experiencing that — I can help. Please restart your modem/router by unplugging its power for 30 seconds, then plug it back in and wait 2–3 minutes for it to fully boot; let me know what the lights show and whether the connection improves.

I already restarted the router about 3 hours ago — it took ~10 minutes to come back up, my smart cameras went offline during that, and it didn’t fix the intermittent drops. Please check for any area outage on your end before more device troubleshooting; I do have an ethernet cable and my phone hotspot available as temporary options.

Thanks — could I have your full name and account number so I can check for area outages now? Also, are all devices affected (including any wired/ethernet connection), or only Wi-Fi devices, and is your phone hotspot available as a temporary workaround?

Name: Sam Torres, account TN-884721. The drops are affecting Wi‑Fi devices (laptop and smart cameras); I haven’t tried a wired/ethernet connection yet. My phone hotspot is available as a temporary workaround.

Thanks, Sam — I see an active outage affecting Neighborhood zone 4B caused by fiber trunk line degradation; our network team is working on it with an estimated resolution in 2–4 hours. Please use your phone hotspot as a temporary workaround (or try a wired ethernet connection once the network is restored for a more stable link); would you like me to schedule a technician visit now in case the issue remains after the outage window?

result.status: Network team actively working on resolution
